title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a coding-agent implementation brief after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Change Scope · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Which action demonstrates Change Scope without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

content.text: Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Change Scope condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

options.text: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a coding-agent implementation brief.
A total rewrite is implied whenever existing code is low quality. This does not resolve change scope · changed case.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Change Scope evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Change Scope assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Order work so Change Scope has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Implementation Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.title: Implementation Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff. Artifact under review: a coding-agent implementation brief.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Implementation Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Implementation Evidence record says a coding-agent implementation brief looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Implementation Evidence without its stated preconditions, omit the a coding-agent implementation brief trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Implementation Evidence for a coding-agent implementation brief.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Implementation Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed review case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Implementation Evidence through the concrete a coding-agent implementation brief case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
